sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 30

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 30:1
items.html:
(to the question) Dam Nidah [menstrual blood]
The MAN [Mayin Nukvin] — which are the discernments of Galgalta ve Eynaim of the lower one, receiving their correction in the vessels of the Yesod and womb [Rechem] of the upper one, which are the discernments of the AHP of the upper one that were returned in Gadlut [greatness] — have in that Yesod and womb two seals [Chotamot]. The first, from ZA, by which it became a boundary, which is the secret of the measure of engraving in the Yesod — see Answer 9 — made there in the secret of the bent fire [Esh Nichpefet]. The second is the discernment of the seal [Chotam] in the secret that its letters also spell Chomat [wall], which is the secret of the guarding from the discernment of the AHP of Gadlut that were returned to her, which she receives with the NHY of Ima — the secret of the new NHY that came out in Ima by the power of the inclusion of the Nukva there, at the time she received from Ima the discernment of her Mochin of Gadlut. As is known, these return to the Nukva as a clothing for her Mochin; and although those vessels are from Ima, from the respect of the Lights in them they belong to the Nukva, for they are the discernment of the level that pertains only to the Nukva and not to Ima. Therefore the illumination of the three names Ehyeh in the NHY of Ima is Chotam [seal] in Gematria; and this seal is the secret of the wall and the guarding that come there from the Mochin of Gadlut. And this second seal is not a measure of sinking [Shekia], like the first seal, but a measure of protrusion [Blita] (see Answer 9); and this is the secret of the Ateret Yesod [crown of Yesod] of the Nukva, which is only a discernment of protrusion — and understand this well.
